file_name: XACCT Technologies, Inc.SUPPORT AND MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 "Product Maintenance" means the Product updates and revisions that are available from XACCT and selected by Licensee, as further referenced herein and the attachments hereto as may be amended from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 "Product Maintenance" means the Product enhancements that are mandated by XACCT, as further referenced herein and the attachments hereto as may be amended from time to time. Licensee has no option to select the enhancements.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Product Maintenance' allows the Licensee to select updates and revisions. The modified definition states that Product Maintenance refers to 'Product enhancements' that are mandated by XACCT, and the Licensee has no choice in selecting them. This introduces a contradiction in the text regarding the Licensee's control over Product Maintenance, leading to uncertainty in enforcement of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee may purchase the level of Product Maintenance and Support Services set forth in Attachment 1 to this Agreement. XACCT will provide the Product Maintenance and Support Services purchased by Licensee subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the License Agreement. Fees shall be payable within thirty (30) days of invoice which shall be exclusive of any applicable local, state, federal, use, excise, value added, GST or other taxes imposed on the fees payable for the Product Maintenance and Support Services which shall be the responsibility of Licensee.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Product Maintenance' allows the Licensee to select updates and revisions. The changed definition states that Product Maintenance refers to 'Product enhancements' that are mandated by XACCT, and the Licensee has no choice in selecting them. This change directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'Licensee may purchase the level of Product Maintenance and Support Services set forth in Attachment 1 to this Agreement', as the licensee no longer has a choice to 'purchase the level' if the maintenance is mandated.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 XACCT will provide reasonable commercial efforts to provide the appropriate solutions for reported Incidents. Initial response times for reported Incidents are as set forth in Attachment 1.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 XACCT will guarantee solutions for reported Service Requests. Initial response times for reported Incidents are as set forth in Attachment 1.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to 'Incidents' and using 'reasonable commercial efforts' to provide solutions. The modified text replaces 'Incidents' with 'Service Requests' and changes 'reasonable commercial efforts' to 'guarantee solutions'. Since Incident definition is kept in 1.2, while Service Request is not being defined, this change suggests a higher level of commitment from XACCT for Service Request resolution than what is suggested in the contract for Incidents. While the definition of Incident is still kept, the modification here can suggest that Service Requests are a different type of request that have a guaranteed solution, while Incident's do not have
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 "Incident" means a single, discrete, malfunction or other problem which may require more than one (1) response before it is closed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Incidents' with 'Service Requests' and changes the commitment level from 'reasonable commercial efforts' to 'guarantee solutions'. The definition of 'Incident' in 1.2 specifies a discrete malfunction requiring potentially multiple responses. The original contract only committed to 'reasonable commercial efforts' for incidents. The modification commits to 'guarantee solutions' for 'Service Requests'. Because 'Service Request' is undefined, and Incident is defined, this implies Service Requests are different from Incidents, and have a higher solution guarantee, creating a conflict in the level of service and a misaligned definition of Incident to Service Request.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 In order for Licensee to receive the Support Services referenced above, Licensee must: (a) Appoint Designated Support Contact(s), trained and qualified, who will maintain the integrity of the&sbsp;Product and who will act as Licensee's liaison for all technical communications with XACCT. The number of Designated Support Contact(s) will be determined by the level of Support Services purchased by Licensee set forth in Attachment 1referenced in Section 2. Names of Designated Support Contact(s) must be provided to XACCT prior to initial contact with the XACCT support center. All technical communications by Licensee to XACCT shall only be made by the Designated Support Contact(s). All information and materials provided to Licensee by XACCT pursuant to this Agreement will be routed to the Designated Support Contact(s). Licensee may change the Designated Support Contact(s) upon written notice to XACCT
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 In order for Licensee to receive the Premium Support Services referenced above, Licensee must: (a) Appoint Designated Support Personnel, trained and qualified, who will maintain the integrity of the&sbsp;Product and who will act as Licensee's liaison for all technical communications with XACCT. The number of Designated Support Contact(s) will be determined by the level of Support Services purchased by Licensee set forth in Attachment 1referenced in Section 2. Names of Designated Support Contact(s) must be provided to XACCT prior to initial contact with the XACCT support center. All technical communications by Licensee to XACCT shall only be made by the Designated Support Contact(s). All information and materials provided to Licensee by XACCT pursuant to this Agreement will be routed to the Designated Support Contact(s). Licensee may change the Designated Support Personnel upon written notice to XACCT
perturbation.explanation: The text originally refers to 'Support Services', 'Designated Support Contact(s)' where there is already a definition for Designated Support Contact in 1.1. The modified text introduces 'Premium Support Services' and 'Designated Support Personnel', while still referencing Designated Support Contact(s). The introduction of these new terms, without defining them, creates ambiguity regarding the level of support being referenced and the role of these personnel. Are Premium Support Services, another type of service? Is Designated Support Personnel a different entity from Designated Support Contact(s)? This leads to potential disputes over the scope of services and who is authorized to communicate with XACCT
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 "Designated Support Contact " means Licensee's employee who is authorized to contact the XACCT support center.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces 'Designated Support Personnel' while the contradicted text defines 'Designated Support Contact'. The changed text still references 'Designated Support Contact(s)' in the same paragraph, implying a difference between the two terms, which creates ambiguity and contradicts the existing definition in section 1.1. If 'Designated Support Personnel' are now authorized to contact XACCT, then section 1.1 is no longer correct as it defines authorized personnel as 'Designated Support Contact', which leads to a contradiction.
